conflicts.label: Iran 2026 (Day 65)
conflicts.note: Operation Epic Fury — Day 65. Ceasefire Day 25 (indefinite extension Day 12); Trump's 'hostilities terminated' WPR letter to Hill (May 1) remains contested. Three U.S. CSGs in CENTCOM AOR + LHA Tripoli — first time since OIF 2003 (Lincoln Arabian Sea blockade ops; Ford Eastern Mediterranean per USNI Apr 27 — dashboard 'Red Sea' label updated; Bush off Africa heading to 5th Fleet). CENTCOM Cdr Adm. Cooper visited USS Tripoli (LHA-7) in Arabian Sea May 2 (@CENTCOM X) — strategic-communication signal timed to Iran's 14-pt counter-proposal delivery. Hormuz dual blockade hardening; 48 vessels redirected cumulative (CBS via CENTCOM May 2; +3 in past 20 hours). Iran parliament 12-article 'Sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz' law poised for chamber vote — bans Israeli vessels permanently; rial-payment requirement; 20% cargo confiscation penalty. Brent $108.17 / WTI $101.94 (Friday close, Sun carryforward); AAA gas $4.446 (+48.7% vs $2.99 baseline; +5.4¢ d/d, ~30¢/wk). Iran 14-pt counter-proposal delivered via Pakistan May 2 (Deputy FM Gharibabadi) — 30-day deadline, end of war 'on all fronts including Lebanon,' troop withdrawal, blockade lift, frozen-assets release; Trump 'cannot imagine' acceptable; IRGC May 3: 'impossible military operation or a bad deal.' State Dept fast-tracks $8.6B arms sales to Israel/Qatar/Kuwait/UAE (Rubio emergency waiver). Lebanon front structurally widening: IDF May 3 issued evacuation orders for towns NORTH of Litani — first major geographic expansion since Apr 17 truce. Lebanese MoPH cumulative 2,659 killed / 8,183 wounded since Mar 2 (+41 in 24h May 2). Iran Foundation of Martyrs Apr 19 cumulative 3,468 (STALE — 14 days); Iran MoH 3,375; HRANA 3,636. ~4.2M displaced (Iran 3.2M + Lebanon ~1M). U.S. KIA 15 (all-cause; Pentagon-official 13 combat-only); WIA 415. Cumulative U.S. cost midpoint $54.345B; range $25B (Pentagon Comptroller Hurst Apr 29 official; ~$416M/day average) → ~$1T (CSIS upper bound incl. economic spillover). Day 65 places this conflict between OIF initial combat phase (~26 days) and Operation Allied Force (Kosovo, 78 days). 3-CSG concentration last seen during OIF 2003. WPR 60-day mark passed May 1 without congressional authorization — first such administration claim of 'hostilities terminated' to circumvent the WPR clock since the law's 1973 enactment.

conflicts.label: Iraq 2003 (Day 21)
conflicts.note: Operation Iraqi Freedom — "shock and awe" + ground invasion. 300,000 troops deployed.

conflicts.label: Libya 2011 (Day 21)
conflicts.note: Operation Odyssey Dawn / Unified Protector — NATO no-fly zone. Limited US role.

conflicts.label: Gulf 1991 (Day 21)
conflicts.note: Operation Desert Storm — 38-day air campaign + 100-hour ground war. 700,000 coalition troops.

sources: CRS Reports R44116 (Iraq), RL33110 (Gulf War), R41725 (Libya). Costs inflation-adjusted to 2026 dollars using BLS CPI-U.
